figures.caption: Figure 4: Schematic depiction of the estimation procedure.
Figure 19: Estimated extremal index Θ̂+ of R̃k against tail-quantile α.

Figure 18: Autocorrelation of absolute values residual time series of rotated return modes |R̃k |, against time lag. The first value for each mode is plotted at a lag of 1s. Markers are evenly spaced for better readability, data frequency is 1s.
Figure 20: Residuals of mode 2, constant 99.9% quantile and the local rolling 99.9% quantile calculated over the last 10000s.
Figure 15: Probability densities gEV γ,b,a for maxima of the first 5 modes, γ̂+, σ̂+ estimated at a threshold of u = 15 with POT.

Figure 14: Autocorrelation of absolute values of rotated return modes |Rk |, against time lag. The first value for each mode is plotted at a lag of 1s. Markers are evenly spaced for better readability, data frequency is 1s.
Figure 25: Q-Q plots of fitted GPD with increasing local tail-quantile threshold, positive tail.

Figure 16: Probability densities gEV γ,b,a for maxima of the first 5 modes, γ̂+, σ̂+ estimated at a threshold of u = 15 with POT in comparison with the empirical probability densities obtained via block maxima.

Figure 3: Schematic depiction of clustering of peaks over the threshold. Top: Independent, unclustered exceedances. Θ̂ = 1. Bottom: Exceedances derived from the time series above, but occurring in clusters of two. Θ̂ = 0.68.

Figure 8: Probability density function for aggregated returns in a time interval of 50 days in 2014, with return horizon 1s. Obtained empirically (black line) and generated by a random matrix model (red circles). Left: Ordinate logarithmically scaled. Right: Ordinate linearly scaled. Adapted from Ref. [14].
Figure 7: Left and right tails of the probability density functions for the modes (rotated and rescaled) referring to the five largest eigenvalues and the aggregation of all modes.

Figure 10: Naive estimation of tail shape parameter without declustering extreme values. Top: Estimated γ̂+ against tail-quantile α. Middle: Estimated γ̂+ against threshold u. Standard errors according to (17). Bottom: NRMSD of estimation.

Figure 22: Nonstationary probability density gEV γ,b(t),a of the second mode plotted for forty consecutive trading days. Estimated via local rolling 99.9% quantile calculated over the last 10000s. Time resolution is 1s, the probability density function is normalized in each time step t .
Figure 6: Left and right tails of the probability density functions for rotated returns referring to the five largest eigenvalues, without the rescaling 1/ √ Λk .
Figure 1: Probability density function gEV γ,b,a(x ) for γ = 0 and special cases γ = −1/2 < 0, γ = 1/2 > 0.
Figure 23: Q-Q plots of fitted GPD with increasing threshold, positive tail.

Figure 11: Naive estimation of tail shape parameter without declustering extreme values. Top: Estimated γ̂− against tail-quantile α. Middle: Estimated γ̂− against threshold −u. Standard errors according to (17). Bottom: NRMSD of estimation.

Figure 5: Top left: Spectral density of the financial correlation matrix (∆t = 1s) of 2014. Top right and bottom: Largest three normalized eigenvectors visibly corresponding to the market, the Energy sector and the Utility sector of the GICS [41].

Figure 21: Estimation of positive tail shape parameter γ̂+ with a rolling threshold determined by the local tail-quantile α over 10000s. Top: Estimated γ̂, standard errors according to (17). Bottom left: NRMSD of estimation. Bottom right: Estimated extremal index Θ̂+.
Figure 17: Top: Volatility profile of the second mode V2 referring to the Energy sector. Bottom: Volatility profile of the stock returns of AAPL VAAPL.
Figure 24: Q-Q plots of fitted GPD with increasing threshold, negative tail.
Figure 12: Estimated extremal index Θ̂+ against tail-quantile α.
Figure 13: Estimated extremal index Θ̂− against tail-quantile α.
Figure 2: Probability density function gGPD γ,σ (x ) for γ = 0 and special cases γ = −1/2 < 0, γ = 1/2 > 0.
Figure 9: Binned exceedances for both tails of the first mode with tail-quantile α = 0.005 together with the theoretical density function gGPD
